Reconciled the frontend discovery boundary from the injected planning inputs. The MVP frontend scope is limited to a simple React todo UI that supports creating, viewing, editing, completing, uncompleting, and deleting todo items; ownership stays within the frontend lane and assumes a backend todo CRUD API plus basic validation rules as downstream dependencies only. Discovery produces no durable frontend artifacts or outbound handoffs in this task.
Execution finished with status ready_for_bundle_review.

Reconciled middleware discovery scope from the injected planning inputs and captured the integration boundary, external dependencies, open contract questions, and downstream handoff needs as an assertion for later phases.
Execution finished with status ready_for_bundle_review.

Backend discovery scope is bounded to MVP todo CRUD service behavior for a small React client: create, list, update text, toggle completion, and delete persisted todo items across reloads. Out of scope at discovery are auth, multi-user tenancy, pagination/filtering/sorting, realtime sync, offline conflict handling, analytics, background jobs, and optimization-heavy architecture. Backend boundary is a simple API plus persistence layer owned by this lane; frontend integration depends on a shared API contract asset later, but discovery should only identify that surface, not define it. Main backend unknowns to resolve in design are transport/API shape, persistence mechanism consistent with 'keep the stack simple', validation/error-depth expectations, todo item schema details, and local development/reset workflow. Shared contract surfaces likely owned or co-owned later are the todo entity shape, CRUD request/response envelopes, error semantics, and persistence-related handoff expectations. Input detail was partially truncated in the injected planning context, but the supplied summary/objective/success-criteria content was sufficient to complete discovery scope capture.
Execution finished with status ready_for_bundle_review.
No backend worker tasks are scheduled for this design lane because the exact output contract authorizes no final lane outputs and no outbound handoffs in this phase.

Execution finished with status ready_for_bundle_review.

Reconciled the MVP React todo frontend design from the injected discovery inputs into a frontend-owned design package at the assertion level. The reconciled scope covers a single simple todo surface with list, empty, loading, and error states; create flow with required non-empty text validation and reset-on-success behavior; item-level complete/uncomplete toggles; edit behavior constrained to a single active item with save/cancel and unchanged-value no-op handling; delete with explicit user intent and removal from the rendered list after success; and frontend consumption constraints that treat API shape, persistence, and integration contracts as externally owned. Frontend-owned validation criteria are limited to UI-observable behavior: the user can view existing todos, add a new todo, edit an existing todo, mark complete/incomplete, delete an item, see empty/loading/error feedback, and avoid conflicting local interaction states during in-flight item actions.
Execution finished with status ready_for_bundle_review.
